explanation: It acts as the 'Funding Bucket' for specific line items on a contract.
A formalized process and custom clause used for handling disputes without litigation.
The contracting officer responsible for administering the contract after it has been awarded.
A specific type of contracting for professional architectural and engineering services.
An announcement used before an RFP is issued to explore what solutions are available in the market.
A contractor's final, most competitive pricing and technical proposal submitted after negotiations conclude.
A simplified contracting vehicle used to fill anticipated repetitive needs for supplies or services.
A contracting vehicle containing terms and clauses applying to future contracts between parties.
Money spent by a contractor specifically for preparing, submitting, and supporting a proposal cost.
The documentation detailing the logic and math of how the estimated costs for a proposal were derived.
A percentage fee collected by an agency when the government uses their specific contract vehicle.
A unique 5-character ID number used to identify specific vendor entities (e.g., a specific Navy Hospital).
A separate set of government accounting rules used to determine and combine costs.
A report delivered under the contract that describes and defines exactly how the deliverable should look.
Indicates that the contractor (not the government) is responsible for providing the material or equipment to build.
The master ecFR where all rules and clauses live, encompassing more than just what Acquisition.gov shows.
Information that requires safeguarding or dissemination controls but is not strictly classified.
A numbering system used to logically identify, price, and track specific deliverables on a contract.
The standardized framework guiding the effective management and lifecycle of contracts.
The unified cybersecurity and FedRAMP rules standard for future government acquisitions.
The government's official assessment report card on how well you performed on the contract.
A system of compliance for buy-side activities evaluating how effectively a contractor spends Government funds.
The main Contracting Officer (also called KO) who actually signs and initiates the contract.
Products or services that are ready-made and available for sale to the general public.
A contract type that provides a base fee and an extra subjective award fee based on performance.
A formal proposal submitted to modify the design or engineering specifications of an existing contract.
The ideal order quantity a company should purchase to minimize total inventory costs.
A contract provision that allows for the upward or downward change of the price on the agreement.
The agency that actually pays you, utilizing form DD250 to process those payments.
The government contracting system used for prioritizing defense-related contracting work.
An order for supplies placed against an established contract vehicle.
A standardization document that defines exactly what data is required of a contractor.
The computer-to-computer exchange format often used with systems like WAWF to make electronic payments.
An identifier for items on an exhibit, operating as a sub-level to a CLIN.
Designates where the product is accepted (Origin or Destination) and who holds liability during transit.
The law that makes certain government data and records free to use and accessible to the public.
A contract type where the price is not subject to any adjustment based on the contractor's actual costs.
A contract providing for adjusting profit and establishing a final price based on a target cost formula.
The overhead cost incurred for the general management and administration of the business unit as a whole.
A multiple-award contract used to procure information technology products across multiple agencies.
The highest contracting official responsible for managing the contracting activity for an entire agency.
The method used for the sealed bidding process of formal contracting.
A contractor's R&D costs that are not sponsored by or required in the performance of a government contract.
Components of a system for which the times to design and manufacture are the longest.
Contracts made with two or more contractors for the same or similar supplies or services.
The contractor's system for planning, controlling, and accounting for the acquisition and use of material.
A cooperative agreement written between parties to cooperatively work together on an agreed upon project.
Evaluates conflicts based on whose side you are on: working for the Govt, or working with the Govt.
The sell-side business process detailing how a contractor actually sells stuff and receives payment.
Costs not previously identified as direct material or labor, such as travel costs or inter-company work transfers.

distractors: Office of Financial Planning and Pricing
explanation: Provides overall direction for government-wide procurement policies, regulations, and procedures.
Not a binding offer, but a high-level planning number of what a project will generally cost.
A solicitation requesting contractors on a multiple-award vehicle to submit a proposal for a specific task.
A short, descriptive document stating what the government wants as the end objective, letting the contractor propose the 'how'.
The specific time frame during which the contractor is expected to deliver the required goods or services.
The internal government document that initiates the procurement process and secures the funding.
A contractor's request to change the contract price or terms due to an unanticipated change or government action.
An arrangement where companies team together to attack a proposal and act as a prime/sub relationship.
A requirement for contractors to provide certified cost or pricing data when negotiating contracts that exceed the threshold.
A specialized contracting officer who is specifically authorized to terminate contracts.
A standardized document structure that formats every solicitation section by a specific letter or number.
A contract action where the terms, specifications, or price are not fully agreed upon before work begins.
The comprehensive conceptual framework and terminology for the contract management profession.
A contract type where the contractor receives reimbursement for allowable costs plus a fixed fee negotiated at inception.
A contract type that provides for an initially negotiated fee to be adjusted later by a formula based on total allowable costs.
The executive branch department responsible for providing the military forces needed to deter war and protect the country.

distractors: Defense Facility Assessment and Review System
explanation: A supplement to the FAR that provides specific acquisition regulations for the Department of Defense.

distractors: Department of Contract Assessment and Analysis
explanation: The agency responsible for performing financial and accounting audits for the DoD and other federal entities.

distractors: Department of Contract Maintenance and Administration
explanation: The DoD agency that works directly with defense contractors to help ensure that goods and services are delivered on time and within cost.
The primary set of rules and guidelines governing all executive agency acquisitions in the US federal government.

correct_answer: Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program
distractors: Federal Resource Allocation and Management Protocol
Federal Record and Administration Management Program
explanation: A government-wide program providing a standardized approach to security assessment and authorization for cloud products.
A fixed-price contract that includes an award fee for exceptional performance, though it is relatively uncommon.
The executive department responsible for advancing the national, economic, and energy security of the United States.
The federal executive department responsible for developing and executing federal laws related to farming, forestry, and food.
The executive department concerned with promoting economic growth, creating jobs, and encouraging sustainable development.
The agency of the federal government that establishes policy for, administers, and coordinates most federal assistance to education.

correct_answer: Department of Health and Human Services
explanation: The cabinet-level department with the goal of protecting the health of all Americans and providing essential human services.
The federal executive department responsible for public security, roughly comparable to the interior or home ministries of other countries.

correct_answer: Department of Housing and Urban Development
explanation: The executive department responsible for national policy and programs that address America's housing needs and improve communities.
The federal executive department responsible for the enforcement of the law and administration of justice in the United States.
The federal executive department primarily responsible for making and conducting foreign policy.
The federal executive department responsible for the management and conservation of most federal land and natural resources.
The federal cabinet department concerned with transportation.
An independent agency that helps manage and support the basic functioning of federal agencies, often acting as the government's landlord.
The supreme audit institution of the federal government that provides investigative services for the United States Congress.
The general industry abbreviation for businesses and processes related to fulfilling contracts for the government.
Property in the possession of, or directly acquired by, the Government and subsequently furnished to the contractor for performance.
A contract type that provides for an indefinite quantity of supplies or services during a fixed period of time.
A commercial contracting vehicle establishing the core terms and conditions for future transactions between a buyer and seller.
A reliability metric measuring the predicted elapsed time between inherent failures of a mechanical or electronic system.
A metric indicating the average time it takes to repair a failed piece of equipment and return it to normal operation.

correct_answer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
explanation: A physical sciences laboratory and non-regulatory agency that promotes U.S. innovation and industrial competitiveness by advancing standards.

distractors: National Association of Independent Contracting Systems
explanation: The standard used by Federal statistical agencies in classifying business establishments for the purpose of collecting and analyzing data.

distractors: Official Command of Naval United Systems
Overseas Contracting Operations and National Unified Strategy
explanation: Refers to any location outside the 48 contiguous U.S. states and the District of Columbia, including Alaska, Hawaii, and foreign countries.

correct_answer: Office of the Secretary of Defense
explanation: The principal staff element of the Secretary of Defense in the exercise of policy development, planning, and resource management.
A group or department within a business or agency that defines and maintains standards for project management within the organization.
A solicitation used to gather market research and industry feedback before a formal solicitation is written.
A solicitation used in simplified acquisition procedures to obtain pricing, delivery, and other info from vendors.
A formal solicitation asking contractors to submit detailed proposals outlining how they will solve a problem and at what cost.
The official U.S. Government system (SAM.gov) that consolidates the capabilities of multiple vendor registration and tracking systems.
A standardized document prescribed by federal agencies for government-wide use.
A document that explicitly details the specific tasks, deliverables, and timelines a contractor must execute.
An item identifier used to separately track or price a subset of a parent Contract Line Item Number (CLIN).
A contract type providing for acquiring supplies or services on the basis of direct labor hours at fixed hourly rates and actual cost of materials.
An order for services placed against an established contract or with Government sources.
The exercise of the Government's right to completely or partially terminate a contract because of the contractor's actual or anticipated failure to perform.
The exercise of the Government's right to completely or partially terminate a contract when it is in the Government's interest.

distractors: Total Cost of Procurement and Deployment Act
Terms of Contractual Obligations and Pricing Data Act
explanation: A law requiring contractors to submit certified cost or pricing data to the government when negotiating contracts above a certain threshold.
The federal government of the republic of fifty states that constitute the United States.
A secure web-based system for electronic invoicing, receipt, and acceptance for DoD contracts.
A hierarchical decomposition of the total scope of work to be carried out by the project team to accomplish the project objectives.
